reasoning: There are no human vaccines approved or in late-stage clinical trials for Yersinia pseudotuberculosis. Research using attenuated Y. pseudotuberculosis is focused on creating vaccines for plague (Y. pestis), which remains in preclinical/early stages. An animal vaccine (Yersiniavax) exists for deer in New Zealand, but the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' focuses on human pathogens and is heavily patrolled. Even if an editor mistakenly adds the animal vaccine, it is highly likely to be reverted. Given the strict policies of forecasting platforms against Wikipedia vandalism and the lack of any actual human vaccine on the horizon, the probability of the Wikipedia page stating 'Yes' on the resolution dates is extremely low. Probabilities slightly increase over time to account for the infinitesimally small chance of an experimental vaccine progressing or a persistent Wikipedia edit slipping through.
